HMW-V10: Driver Milestone Updates — Vendor & Admin Visibility

How might we surface driver-reported milestones (Arrived at Pickup / Departed / Arrived at Delivery) in the vendor and admin views — so dispatchers can track live FM execution without drilling into the activity log every time?
Context (TODO-050): This is the third leg of the platform: Admin creates trip → Vendor dispatches driver → Driver executes in WeChat (TODO-049). When a driver taps "Arrived at Pickup" in WeChat, that event must: (1) update the job's state in shared storage, (2) append an activity log entry with "Driver Zhang Wei" as actor, (3) be visible in real time in both the Vendor app and Admin slide-out. The challenge is displaying this in the existing job detail UI without adding an entirely new data surface — the current layout is Status Bar → Driver & Vehicle → Route → Cargo → Proof → Activity Log.

Key tensions: (A) The activity log already captures all events — is a new milestone strip redundant? (B) Dispatchers at a 3PL office need to know where a truck is right now, not just scroll to the bottom of a log. (C) The admin slide-out is 380px wide — a horizontal milestone strip must be compact. (D) The milestone sequence (Arrived Pickup → Departed → Arrived Delivery) is FM-only. Non-FM services don't have this concept.

Option A: Named Actor in Activity Log Only

Low effort, minimal change
Driver WeChat actions appear in the activity log with "Driver Zhang Wei" as the actor — distinct from "HaleSun" (vendor) and "Ops Admin" (admin). No new UI section. The log becomes the single source of truth for all actors in the system. Dispatchers who want to know where the truck is must open the job detail and scroll to the activity log. An optional refinement: a small monochrome actor badge (DRV / OPS / VDR) prefixes each log entry, making it scannable in dense logs without color or weight changes.
Pros
• Zero new UI surface — log already exists
• Actor names are self-explanatory
• Works identically in vendor full-page and admin 380px slide-out
• No new data model concept
Cons
• Dispatcher must scroll to log to know current location — not glanceable
• In a busy log (10+ entries) driver milestones are buried
• No indication on the job list that execution has progressed
• Easy to miss if vendor only checks Status column
Teleport OS Vendor My Jobs Fleet HaleSun Logistics
HS
← My Jobs | J03 FM Trucking FM

DRV Arrived at Pickup · Driver Zhang Wei
15 May · 08:47
OPS Job started · HaleSun
15 May · 07:54
OPS Assigned Zhang Wei + 粤B·12345 · HaleSun
15 May · 07:30
OPS Job created · Ops Admin
14 May · 16:20
↑ When driver taps "Departed" in WeChat, a new DRV entry appears here automatically.

Option B: FM Milestone Progress Strip on Job Detail ⭐ Recommended

Recommended
A compact 3-step progress strip is added to FM job detail pages, between the Driver & Vehicle section and the Route section. It shows the three FM execution milestones (Arrived at Pickup → Departed → Arrived at Delivery) with timestamps as each is hit. The strip is 3 columns with connector lines — no cards, no padding excess. Width: full content width (~928px vendor, or 348px in admin slide-out). The activity log still records every milestone event with "Driver [Name]" as actor — the strip is additive, not a replacement. Dispatchers get a glanceable execution state; the log provides the audit trail. For the admin slide-out (380px), the strip stacks vertically into a compact 3-row list with right-aligned timestamps, rather than the horizontal 3-column layout used in the full vendor detail page.
Pros
• Glanceable execution state without scrolling to log
• FM-specific section: non-FM jobs are unaffected
• Fits both vendor full-page and admin slide-out (adaptive layout)
• Colors use existing design tokens: green for done, blue for current, gray for pending
• Timestamp + actor name in each step ties back to WeChat source
Cons
• Adds a new UI section (FM jobs only — limited blast radius)
• Milestone data must be stored on Job type (3 new optional fields)
• Horizontal strip may feel spacious at narrow widths — needs truncation
Vendor App — Full Page (960px) — After driver taps "Arrived at Pickup"

Option C: Current Milestone as Sub-line in Job List "Where" Column

List-first visibility
Driver milestones are surfaced in the My Jobs list view rather than the job detail page. For In Progress FM jobs, the "Where" sub-line changes from "Zhang Wei · 粤B·12345" (dispatch state) to "● Arrived Pickup · 08:47" (execution state) once the driver reports a milestone. No new section on the detail page. This gives dispatchers list-level visibility — they can scan 20 jobs and see which drivers are on-site, en route, etc. — without opening each job. The sub-line space is limited (~200px), so the milestone must be abbreviated.
Pros
• List-level visibility — no detail page required
• Uses existing "Where" sub-line real estate (no new columns)
• Dispatcher can monitor multiple jobs at once
• Most efficient for dispatchers tracking a fleet
Cons
• Sub-line switches from driver+plate → milestone, losing driver identity
• Limited space for milestone label + timestamp + driver name
• "Where" column already carries route + driver info — adding milestone overloads it
• Non-FM rows must show nothing, creating inconsistency in a mixed-service list
• No progression context (only current state, not all 3 milestones)

45m ago

T-2026-042 TikTok EC
HKIA — HK Airport
MAWB 113-54321678
15 May
14:00
 Pending Pending

✓ Verdict: Option B (Milestone Strip) + Named Actor in Log

Recommended approach: Add an FM Execution milestone strip on the job detail page (vendor full-page and admin slide-out), plus use "Driver [Name]" actor label in all activity log entries triggered by WeChat actions.

Why not A alone: The activity log is the right audit trail, but it's not glanceable. A dispatcher tracking 10 FM jobs in parallel needs to open each job and scroll down to see current execution state. The milestone strip solves this without any new navigation.

Why not C alone: The "Where" sub-line already carries driver+plate. Adding a milestone overwrites that data, creating a confusing flip-flop. Additionally, list-level is not the right place for this: the sub-line space is too narrow for three-state milestone progression. Option C is useful for dispatchers, but it works best as a complement to B, not a replacement.

Implementation notes: (1) Add three optional fields to the Job type: milestoneArrivedPickup?: string, milestoneDeparted?: string, milestoneArrivedDelivery?: string (ISO timestamps). (2) The strip renders only when job.service.code === 'FM'. (3) Admin slide-out renders the strip as a vertical compact list (3 rows, right-aligned timestamps) rather than horizontal — same data, different layout constraint. (4) In the My Jobs list, the "Where" sub-line shows the current milestone in parentheses when present: "Zhang Wei · 粤B·12345 · ● Arrived Pickup" — milestone appended, not replaced, so driver identity is preserved.

Open question: Should "Departed" be required before "Arrived Delivery" (enforced in WeChat sequencing), or can a driver skip directly to "Arrived Delivery" if they forgot to tap "Departed"? If milestones can be reported out-of-order, the progress strip must handle gaps gracefully (e.g., show both Arrived Pickup and Arrived Delivery green with Departed still gray).
